claim: The spec mounts the OrkyEntryActions region inside each decision-queue-item row, but the F6 hasPane row carries an unguarded onClick={() => focusProject(it.projectRoot)} (DecisionQueuePanel.tsx:164) - only the keydown handler target-guards (:165-168). Every click on a dq-action control (answer, submit, verdict buttons, preview, resume, or the inline decision input) bubbles to the row and fires focusProject, which via focusMruPaneMatch dispatches setActive+setFocusedPane and moves keyboard focus into the matching xterm pane (possibly switching the active workspace) - stealing focus from the just-opened answer input and breaking the primary answer flow on exactly the rows that have a matching pane. REQ-012/CONV-030 covers only key events; no REQ or acceptance vector covers pointer-gesture isolation, so the specified tests (spy-based, mounting the component alone) stay green while the integrated runtime is broken - the test-green/runtime-broken class this repo names.
fix: Add a normative requirement + acceptance vector: activating any dq-action control (click or key) must trigger zero focusProject/setActive/setFocusedPane dispatches - realized by stopPropagation at the actions-region boundary or by target-guarding the row's onClick exactly like its onKeyDown; acceptance: clicking dq-action-answer (and the inline input) inside a hasPane row issues no pane-focus dispatch (spy count 0) and focus stays in the input. propose CONV: A container-level pointer-activation (click) handler MUST be target-guarded, or nested interactive controls MUST stop propagation, so a nested control's activation never also fires the container's own gesture - the pointer twin of CONV-030.
resolution: Resolved by REQ-015 (new) + REQ-012 cross-ref + proposed CONV-041. REQ-015 normatively isolates pointer activation of the whole dq-action region (stopPropagation at the actions-region boundary inside OrkyEntryActions, or a row-onClick target-guard that ignores events originating in the actions slot - correctly NOT the naive target!==currentTarget guard, which the row-body acceptance would catch). Acceptance verified BEHAVIORAL, not spy-only: zero setActive/setFocusedPane at the store seam AND focus stays in dq-action-answer-input receiving typed text on the real rendered tree (e2e or equivalent), plus the row BODY click must still fire exactly one focus-project dispatch. Frozen e2e TEST-366 verified at tests/e2e/decision-queue.spec.ts:99-121: item.click() at :115 clicks the ROW's center point - whether that lands in the future actions region is a layout question unknowable at spec time; the frozen-guard inventory handles it honestly (tests phase MUST confirm the center-click stays on the row body, else amend TEST-366 per CONV-019 atomically), and REQ-015's acceptance pins TEST-366 green. stopPropagation on React synthetic click does stop bubbling to the row's onClick handler at DecisionQueuePanel.tsx:164 (verified unguarded in source).

claim: Wrong pin: REQ-013 and the frozen-guard inventory both assert 'no frozen test pins this comment text' for the ipc-contract.ts:184-185 consumer comment, but frozen TEST-503 (tests/docs-feature-0012.test.ts:69-75) pins exactly that region: not.toContain('no renderer UI consumes these yet'), toMatch(/0012|quick-capture/i), toMatch(/first .*consumer/i), toLowerCase().toContain('submitwork') - the 0012/quick-capture/submitWork matches live only in the sentence REQ-013 orders rewritten. Its repo sweep (:92-107) additionally constrains the REPLACEMENT phrasing (the 'no renderer UI consumes|no renderer consumer' claim class may survive only as the one allowlisted baseline line). An implementer following the spec's false note can break the frozen docs-feature-0012 suite; the inventory's stated grep patterns could never surface this doc guard, so the CONV-023 inventory scope was too narrow.
fix: Correct REQ-013 and the inventory: name tests/docs-feature-0012.test.ts TEST-503 as a byte-relevant constraint on the ipc-contract comment rewrite (the new text must keep matching /first .*consumer/i, keep naming 0012/quick-capture and submitWork, and must not reintroduce any 'no renderer UI consumes|no renderer consumer' phrasing outside the allowlisted baseline line); widen the inventory grep patterns to include the pinned doc-claim literals (e.g. 'consumer' over tests/docs-feature-*.test.ts).
resolution: Resolved by REQ-013 as amended. The false 'no frozen test pins it' claim is retracted; TEST-503 is named as the byte-relevant pin and the edit is scoped ADDITIVE, verified feasible against shipped source: ipc-contract.ts:183-185 holds the pinned sentence ('First renderer consumer: feature 0012's quick-capture modal (submitWork only)' - satisfies /first .*consumer/i, /0012|quick-capture/i, submitwork) followed by the separable stale clause ('the other three actions remain consumer-less until F8/F10') that REQ-013 replaces alone, so the :69-75 pins stay green; the replacement is forbidden from matching the :92-107 sweep regex (/no renderer UI consumes|no renderer consumer/, which sweeps src/** including ipc-contract.ts itself) and from touching the :87-90 allowlisted baseline line (byte-identical, allowlisted at :101-102 by path+registry:addRoot). Acceptance now requires the whole TEST-502/TEST-503 suite green byte-unchanged after the edit. Inventory grep widened per the fix ('first .*consumer|no renderer UI consumes|no renderer consumer|consumer' over tests/docs-feature-*.test.ts).

claim: Answer-time first-open selection can bind the human's decision text to a DIFFERENT escalation than the one the user read. The row's displayed context (status.detail names the escalation id, orky-status.ts:250-252, computed at aggregate time) and the dispatch-time registryDetail pull are separated by an unbounded window in which the open-escalation set can change (feedback apply, the watchdog, another Termhalla window, the running pipeline): 'FIRST escalation with status===open' is positional, not identity-bound, so if the displayed escalation resolves and another opens, the typed decision dispatches against the wrong escalation with NO error path - REQ-003's honest-failure branches cover only pull-failure/no-open/id-less/slug-collision, not changed-target. D2's 'answer resolves THAT escalation; never guesses a target' is violated in this race, and the acceptance vectors never exercise it.
fix: Require the answer UI to render the fetched open escalation's id + reason (from the detail pull) beside the input before dispatch, and re-assert at submit time that the id bound when the input opened still equals the current first-open id - on mismatch, no dispatch plus an actionable 'the open escalation for this feature changed - re-read before answering' message; add an acceptance vector where the detail pull between open and submit returns a different first-open id and orkyResolveEscalation is never called.
resolution: Resolved by REQ-003 as amended (+ Resolved decision #5). Identity binding delivered in full: (1) bind at display time - the first-open selection (mirroring orky-status.ts:219, verified: f.escalations.find(e => e.status==='open') in state.json order) happens ONCE at open, its OrkyEscalationDetail.id captured (mapEscalation id nullable, orky-root-detail.ts:141-153); (2) the bound id + reason rendered beside the input (dq-action-answer-target) before dispatch; (3) submit RE-VERIFIES the bound id against a fresh registryDetail pull and dispatches the bound id explicitly - if no longer open/unmatched/pull-fails, NO dispatch plus the honest 'escalation changed - re-open to answer' message; a still-open bound id dispatches even when no longer positionally first (identity beats position - strictly better than the fix's first-open-equality re-check, which would false-negative). Acceptance covers the exact race vector (open-time ESC-007, submit-time ESC-007 resolved/ESC-009 open: spy count 0 + /changed|re-?open/i), identity-beats-position, all failure branches, and a structural-source-wins fixture against status.detail free text. No silent wrong-target write remains: the dispatch always carries the id the user read.

claim: REQ-007's normative wording promises at most one in-flight dispatch per (entry, async action), but the pinned mechanism - an event-time ref inside useOrkyEntryActions - is per HOOK INSTANCE. The same entry mounts in multiple instances today (one decision-queue drawer per window in this multi-window app) and by explicit design in F10 (REQ-011 mounts the SAME component for the same identity inside OrkyPane), so two instances can concurrently dispatch the same non-idempotent answer - the duplicating double-dispatch REQ-007 exists to prevent. F7's per-feature queue serializes but does not dedupe: both writes run, and on the feedback path both decisions durably queue. The acceptance only exercises a single instance, so the gap is untested by construction.
fix: Either (a) key the in-flight guard in shared store state by (projectRoot, featureSlug, action) so every mounted instance of the same target shares one single-flight gate, adding an acceptance vector with two hook instances of the same target (second gesture is a no-op, spy count 1); or (b) honestly narrow REQ-007's wording to per-mounted-control single-flight and state that cross-instance duplicates rely on F7 serialization plus REQ-009's honest failure of the second write - do not leave the wording promising a guarantee the pinned mechanism cannot deliver.
resolution: Resolved by REQ-007 as amended - fix option (a) plus an honest scope boundary. The gate is now normatively a SHARED seam (module-scope/store-level in-flight registry every mounted useOrkyEntryActions instance consults at gesture time, per-instance refs explicitly banned) keyed by the (projectRoot, featureSlug, action) composite with CONV-039 collision-proof encoding. The two-instance case is genuinely covered: the acceptance's two-instance vector mounts an F8-style AND an F10-style instance for the SAME target and requires exactly ONE dispatch (spy count 1) with BOTH instances rendering dq-action-pending - which forces the registry to be reactive (store-level), not a silent module flag. REQ-010 releases the gate on settle regardless of mount state, closing the leak. The remaining hole (separate OS windows = separate renderer processes, no shared store) is honestly disclaimed as out of mechanism reach, deferring to F7 per-feature serialization + REQ-009 honest surfacing - exactly the (b)-style honesty the finding demanded for whatever the mechanism cannot deliver.

location: 02-spec.md REQ-014 + Verified contract 'The Orky resume command'
claim: The Verified contract's citations do not establish the load-bearing half of the REQ-014 pin: that /orky:resume works as a process ARGV to the claude binary. All three cited Orky docs document it as an in-session slash command only (setup.md:102 says 'All commands are typed in a Claude Code session'; watchdog.md:47-48 is a scheduled Claude routine running the command inside its own session, not a claude re-launcher; design.md:649 likewise). Nothing in either repo spawns claude with a slash command as argv, and Termhalla's only claude-launch precedent types 'claude --resume' - a real CLI flag - into an existing shell (TerminalPane.tsx:88). The commitPane -> ptySpawn -> resolveSpawnSpec mechanism IS genuinely verified end-to-end, but 'claude with initial prompt /orky:resume executes the plugin command' rests on Claude Code's slash-command-in-initial-prompt behavior - an external contract the spec marks as verified-from-source while it is actually from memory. REQ-014's acceptance (deep-equal on the mocked launch args) can never catch a wrong invocation; if expansion does not occur (older CLI, plugin unavailable at that project), the pane opens with a literal/unknown-command prompt. Degradation is visible in a user-owned terminal, so this is a verification-gap finding, not a demonstrated wrong launch.
fix: Verify once against the installed claude CLI (e.g. spawn claude "/orky:status" at an Orky-tracked root and confirm the command expands) and record the observed behavior + minimum CLI version in the Verified contract; otherwise reword the pin to state the external dependency explicitly and add a REQ-014 honesty note that a non-expanding CLI degrades to a visible literal prompt in the user-owned terminal (no silent failure).
resolution: Resolved by REQ-014 as amended + the reworded Verified contract entry ('external contract, stated honestly') - fix option (b), by coordinator decision. The pin now claims exactly what is verified: the argv (launch.command 'claude', launch.args deep-equal ['/orky:resume']) + cwd via the shipped run-on-spawn path, with the slash-command-as-initial-arg expansion explicitly labeled an EXTERNAL Claude Code contract 'neither repo's source can verify', and the two-level honest outcome (auto-run where supported; otherwise a visible interactive session at the correct project the user completes - never a silent failure; missing binary surfaced in-pane by pty-manager.ts:31-36, verified). Copy honesty is enforced by acceptance: the control's accessible name/tooltip must match /terminal|claude|session/i and NONE of /resumed|advanced|dispatched|auto-?run/i. TerminalPane.tsx:88 verified in source (api.ptyWrite 'claude --resume\r' into an already-running pty, gated on resumeAi/autoResumeClaude) and now correctly characterized as a REAL CLI flag typed into a live shell - a different capability, explicitly 'not an argv-expansion precedent'. Docs re-verified: setup.md:104 ('All commands are typed in a Claude Code session' - spec cites :102, two lines off, immaterial), setup.md:118, watchdog.md:47-48, design.md:649 all in-session.

claim: REQ-009's 'every other kind is a DEFINITE failure' over-claims for cli-unparseable on a mutating answer: a gatekeeper/feedback child that ran to completion but printed unparseable stdout may have already completed its durable write (mapCliRunToResult folds exit-0-with-garbage-stdout into cli-unparseable), so branding it a definite non-dispatch is the same false-certainty CONV-015 bans for timeouts and invites the duplicating retry (bounded: a second resolve fails honestly as already-resolved, but on the feedback path a second decision durably queues). CONV-015's letter covers timeout/abort only and F12 shipped the same classification, so this is a precedent-consistency gap rather than a new defect class.
fix: Classify cli-unparseable on a mutating answer as INDETERMINATE (completed child, unconfirmed write) in REQ-009's honesty table, or add an explicit spec rationale for keeping it definite; keep the read-only preview classification unchanged.
resolution: Resolved by REQ-009 as amended: cli-unparseable on a MUTATING answer joins cli-timeout and the renderer-synthesized ipc-failure in the INDETERMINATE class (indeterminate copy, /uncertain|may still|may have|duplicate/i, never definite non-dispatch), while the read-only preview keeps the safe-retry classification WITHOUT duplicate-write wording - exactly the requested fix. Grounding verified in source: mapCliRunToResult reaches the cli-unparseable branches (orky-action-result.ts:42-48,:49-56) only past the timeout branch (:29-36) and regardless of exit code, including exit 0, so a completed child's durable write may have landed. Acceptance vectors cover cli-unparseable answers (indeterminate copy) and cli-unparseable previews (safe-retry, no duplicate warning) plus the full per-kind matrix. NOTE: the repair's accompanying Verified-contract rationale over-claims that the kind ALWAYS means a completed child - spawn-class never-executed failures also fold into cli-unparseable; that inaccuracy (which does not weaken the indeterminate classification) is filed separately as a new LOW finding.

location: 02-spec.md Verified contract 'cli-unparseable semantics' + REQ-009 rationale; src/main/orky/orky-cli-runner.ts:52-67
claim: The FINDING-006 repair grounds the cli-unparseable reclassification on a false verified-from-source pin: the Verified contract states the branch is reached 'only PAST the timeout branch ... so the child ran to completion and a durable write may have landed', and recasts the orky-cli-runner.ts:24 'definite' comment as 'transport-level certainty that the CHILD finished'. Both are wrong: spawn-class failures where the child NEVER executed (callback spawn errno at orky-cli-runner.ts:52-56 and the Windows sync-throw ENAMETOOLONG class at :63-67) resolve {exitCode:null, stdout:'', timedOut:false}, and mapCliRunToResult folds the empty stdout into cli-unparseable (JSON.parse('') throws, orky-action-result.ts:42-48) - so the kind MIXES completed-child-unreadable-report with definite never-ran, and the runner comment's 'definite' means definite NON-execution (nothing written), the opposite of the spec's reading. REQ-009's INDETERMINATE classification survives unweakened (indeterminate is the honest superset over the ambiguous kind), but the false pin can propagate: REQ-009's parenthetical '(the child ran to completion but its report is unreadable - the write may have landed)' invites implementers/copywriters to assert the command COMPLETED, which is false for the never-executed subclass.
fix: Correct the Verified contract entry: cli-unparseable = ANY non-timeout run whose stdout is not the expected JSON object, covering BOTH a completed child with an unreadable report AND a spawn-class never-executed failure (orky-cli-runner.ts:52-67 resolves stdout:'' / timedOut:false into this kind). Keep the INDETERMINATE classification for mutating answers, re-justified as the honest superset over an ambiguous kind, and reword REQ-009's parenthetical so no mandated or example copy asserts the child completed - e.g. 'the CLI report is unreadable; the write may or may not have landed'.
resolution: Fixed (coordinator, pre-freeze prose correction): the Verified-contract networking bullet + REQ-009 amendment note now state cli-unparseable is the honest INDETERMINATE superset covering BOTH completed-unreadable AND never-ran spawn failures (empty stdout -> JSON.parse('')), not a claim the child completed. Classification unchanged; only the over-claiming grounding prose corrected.

claim: REQ-014's resume-in-terminal composes the slice-internal commitPane primitive from a component-level hook (orky-entry-actions.tsx), which required promoting commitPane from a store.ts closure to a public State field (store.ts:550-552, store/types.ts:150-153) — a change not present pre-F8. Every other commitPane caller (launchDir, launchConnection, launchCommand, addEditor, addExplorer, addTerminal, addOrkyPane) wraps it behind a narrowly-scoped store action that fixes most parameters and validates/derives the rest internally; commitPane itself accepts an arbitrary PaneConfig (any kind: terminal with unrestricted launch.command/args, or editor/explorer/orky with an arbitrary root/path) plus placement internals (target, dir, markEditor, position). Exposing it verbatim on State means ANY renderer code that can reach useStore.getState() (not just orky-entry-actions.tsx) can now construct any pane kind with any parameters directly, bypassing the semantic narrowing every existing wrapper enforced. Verified this does not cross a NEW trust boundary today: addExplorer(wsId, targetPaneId, dir, root, splitDir) already accepts an arbitrary root, and launchCommand already accepts an arbitrary {command,args,title} triple with cwd '' — so equivalent-or-greater capability was already reachable pre-F8 by the same caller population (contextIsolation:true/nodeIntegration:false but sandbox:false, window-manager.ts:135; all renderer code is first-party and no untrusted content is rendered in this window today). This is therefore a least-privilege / encapsulation regression (CWE-284-adjacent: broadened internal API surface) rather than a demonstrated new exploit — but it removes a guardrail that would matter the moment any future feature renders less-trusted content in this renderer (e.g. remote markdown/HTML, a plugin surface), and it is easy to miss in future review since commitPane no longer reads as 'store-internal only'.
fix: Replace the raw commitPane exposure with a narrowly-scoped store action mirroring launchCommand's shape, e.g. launchTerminalAt(cwd: string, launch: TerminalLaunch): string that internally calls commitPane with kind:'terminal' fixed and no caller control over pane kind or placement internals (markEditor/position). Remove commitPane from the public State interface (store/types.ts) once resumeInTerminal is rebased onto the new wrapper, restoring the pattern every other commitPane call site already follows.
resolution: Resolved (consolidated re-verification). launchTerminalAt(cwd, launch) is the NARROW named action on State (store/types.ts:155; store.ts:555-561): kind:'terminal', defaultShellId, placement and the F6 workspace-less newWorkspace fallback fixed internally, commitPane composed INSIDE the store; commitPane is OFF the public State interface (present only on SliceDeps, types.ts:221) and the returned store object re-exposes nothing - repo grep confirms the only commitPane consumers are store.ts internals + SliceDeps slices. resumeInTerminal rides the narrow action at gesture time (getState, no envId; orky-entry-actions.tsx:183-189). Pinned by amended frozen TEST-604 + new TEST-619, both green; hashes match test-freeze. The name: launch.title pane-name delta vs launchCommand parity is deliberate and harmless (accepted).

claim: The core's withSingleFlight is DESIGNED to fan out a settled outcome to every caller of the same key (an existing flight is returned AS-IS, i.e. the SAME promise reference, so a second .then() attached to it still fires on settle - this is exactly what TEST-585 exercises directly against the core). But all three hook actions (answerEscalation :112, answerReview :134, preview :149) short-circuit with `if (isInFlight(key)) return` BEFORE ever calling withSingleFlight. This means a caller that arrives while a flight is already registered for that key NEVER reaches withSingleFlight and therefore NEVER attaches a .then(settled => deliver(...)) - so it never receives the settled outcome at all. Within a single mounted instance this is invisible (the FIRST caller's own closure already owns the .then() chain and updates that instance's phase normally), but it defeats the shared-seam design for the exact scenario REQ-007/REQ-011 exist to support: a SECOND mounted instance of the same target (the two-instance case the spec calls out by name - an F8 queue row and F10's OrkyPane row for the same escalation, mounted simultaneously in the same window). That second instance's `busy` correctly flips to true via the reactive isInFlight/subscribeFlights read (so REQ-007's literal acceptance - 'BOTH instances report busy/render dq-action-pending' - is satisfied), but the moment the flight settles, that instance's local `phase` was NEVER set past its initial {status:'idle'} (bindAnswer/toggleAnswer never touch `phase` either), so `showPending` drops back to false and NOTHING renders - no dq-action-result, no dq-action-error - even though a real F7 dispatch happened and durably succeeded or failed. This contradicts 03-plan.md TASK-005's own design statement ('phase transitions ... surfaced identically to every mounted instance consulting the same single-flight key') and is a genuine test-green/runtime-gap: every current REQ-007 acceptance vector (TEST-585/586 at the core seam, the two-instance vector's literal wording) passes because none of them assert on the SECOND instance's post-settle phase, only on its pending render. The gap is currently latent (F8 never mounts two instances of the same target by itself), but the whole reason this module exists as a shared file is for F10 to mount OrkyEntryActions 'unchanged' (REQ-011) for the identical target an F8 row already shows - which is precisely when this silently swallows feedback.
fix: Remove the three redundant `if (isInFlight(key)) return` pre-checks and let every caller (whichever instance, whichever click) call withSingleFlight(key, fn).then(settled => deliver(action, settled)) unconditionally - the core's own dedup (an existing flight is returned AS-IS, fn is never re-invoked) already guarantees exactly one underlying F7 call, so REQ-007's single-dispatch acceptance is unaffected, while now every caller's .then() correctly attaches to the shared promise and receives the settled outcome via its own deliver(), fixing the second-instance case for free. (A harmless side effect: a second click on the SAME instance would now also call setPhase({status:'pending',...}) redundantly before attaching - inconsequential, since it is the same eventual settled value.) Consider adding a two-instance acceptance vector that asserts on the SECOND instance's phase after settle (not only its pending render) so this class of gap cannot silently regress once F10 lands.
resolution: Resolved (consolidated re-verification). All three 'if (isInFlight(key)) return' pre-checks removed: answerEscalation (:127-141), answerReview (:149-157) and preview (:164-172) each route UNCONDITIONALLY through withSingleFlight(key, fn).then(settled => deliver(action, settled)), so a mid-flight second caller's own continuation receives the SETTLED outcome and renders it via its own deliver - while the core's return-existing-flight dedup keeps exactly ONE dispatch (fn never re-invoked; e2e TEST-608's double-Enter still asserts fLog length 1) and each gesture attaches exactly one .then (no double-deliver to the initiator). Pinned by new TEST-615: the core fan-out driven behaviorally (the second caller's .then receives the settled result) + the structural ban on the isInFlight/busy early-return pattern - current source is clean of it. The rendered two-React-instance post-settle state remains honestly deferred to F10 per 04-tests.md ('Named realizations + honest deferrals').

claim: commitPane was, before this diff, a private closure inside the store creator, threaded to slices ONLY via SliceDeps (store/types.ts:221, already the pattern createQuickSlice/createRegistrySlice consume) - i.e. a composition-root-internal primitive trusted callers (launchDir, launchConnection, launchCommand, addTerminal, addEditor, addExplorer, addOrky) each wrap with a narrow, purpose-specific, named action. This diff instead adds `commitPane` directly to the public `State` interface (types.ts:155) and to the object `create<State>(...)` returns (store.ts:552), so it is now callable by ANY renderer code via `useStore.getState().commitPane(...)` with an ARBITRARY PaneConfig - not just orky-entry-actions.tsx. This breaks the established pattern where every other pane-opening entry point on State is a narrowly-scoped, self-documenting action (each hard-codes its own safe subset of PaneConfig fields), and moves invariant enforcement (REQ-014's 'NO envId, no other capability-bearing field' - envId is a real capability, it links to a per-terminal env-vault entry injected on spawn per shared/types.ts:258) from the store's action shape to a source-scan test (TEST-604/TEST-597) that only pins the CURRENT single caller (orky-entry-actions.tsx). Nothing at the store level stops a future consumer of the now-public primitive from calling commitPane with envId or any other PaneConfig shape - the trust boundary that every other launch action encodes structurally has been widened to a raw primitive with no such boundary, precedent-setting for whoever reaches for `commitPane` next.
fix: Prefer a narrowly-scoped named action on State instead of the raw primitive - e.g. `launchTerminalAt(cwd: string, launch: TerminalLaunch): void` (or a feature-named `resumeOrkyInTerminal(projectRoot, featureSlug)`) implemented in store.ts alongside launchDir/launchConnection/launchCommand, composing the private `commitPane` internally with the cwd+launch pairing REQ-014 needs and hard-coding 'no envId' the same way the existing sibling actions hard-code their own safe subset - keeping `commitPane` itself out of the public State surface (SliceDeps only). If the generic primitive is kept public for future-composability reasons, that should be a stated, deliberate architectural decision (e.g. recorded in the plan/ADR) rather than an incidental widening flagged only by the implementer's own code comment.
location: src/renderer/store.ts:550-552 (commitPane exposed on the returned State object) and src/renderer/store/types.ts:150-155 (State.commitPane)
resolution: Merged into FINDING-008 — same commitPane-exposure encapsulation concern, filed by both security(008) and quality(010); 008 canonical.

claim: The single shared `phase` state (answer/preview success-or-failure) is not scoped to 'the action the user is currently looking at'. Opening the answer accordion (`toggleAnswer` -> `bindAnswer`) never resets or touches `phase` - it only sets the separate `binding` state. `errorView` gives `phase.status === 'failure'` unconditional priority over a fresh, more relevant `escalation-unbound` binding message: `phase.status === 'failure' ? {kind: phase.kind, message: phase.message} : (answerOpen && binding not ok) ? {kind:'escalation-unbound', ...} : null`. Concretely: if the user's most recent settled action was a FAILED preview (or a failed prior answer), and they then open (or reopen) the answer accordion whose display-time binding ALSO fails to resolve an open escalation, the stale, unrelated preview/answer failure keeps rendering in dq-action-error (with its own, now-misleading data-error-kind) and the actionable 'escalation-unbound' message never surfaces at all - masked rather than replaced. The reverse mixing is also possible in the brief window right after toggling the accordion open, before a new dispatch is fired: the old success/failure text from a different action is still visible next to the newly-opened, logically unrelated form. In normal single-focus usage the window is often short-lived (any NEW dispatch immediately overwrites `phase` to pending), but the specific 'open the answer form only' path never touches `phase`, so a stale, differently-scoped result can persist indefinitely until the next dispatch.
fix: Scope the rendered result/error to the currently-relevant context: either reset `phase` to idle when `toggleAnswer` opens the form (or on any binding-state transition), or key `errorView`/the success block on `phase.action` matching the control currently in view (e.g. only surface the stale answer/preview phase while its own control is what's active, and give the fresh `escalation-unbound` binding message priority whenever the answer form is open, regardless of a stale unrelated `phase`).
resolution: Resolved (consolidated re-verification) - fix option (a). toggleAnswer calls resetPhase() on OPEN (orky-entry-actions.tsx:327-335; resetPhase :99-102), clearing a stale settled result/failure from another action so it can never mask the fresh escalation-unbound binding message or sit beside the newly opened form; errorView's failure branch is therefore always the form's own fresh context. Pinned behaviorally by e2e TEST-622: (a) a stale preview SUCCESS clears on open; (b) a stale cli-unparseable preview FAILURE (genuine garbage-stdout class) no longer masks - the bound target renders. Noted residual within the LOW class: a flight already in the air at open settles AFTER the reset and renders beside the form - transient and gesture-adjacent, not the indefinite-persistence defect this finding named; accepted.

claim: Detached SUCCESS outcome is silently dropped, contradicting REQ-010 MUST + CONV-034. REQ-010: (quote) the settled outcome MUST NOT update the removed component but MUST still be reported through the store-level toast chokepoint (pushToast) (end quote), closing with (quote) No outcome is ever silently swallowed (end quote); its acceptance pins suppression as the STORE mechanism (the kind !== error early-return) - i.e. a detached success is PUSHED with the suppressible default kind, never dropped at the call site. The implementation deliver() drops it outright (comment at :83: a detached success is dropped - only failures must never be swallowed), so a user who closes the drawer - or whose row leaves the queue on the aggregate refresh - while a non-idempotent answer is in flight gets NO confirmation the write landed, even with toasts enabled. Root cause: 03-plan.md TASK-006 rewrote the frozen requirement (a settled success while unmounted is silently dropped - no toast required by the spec), a claim the spec text contradicts; frozen TEST-605 then pinned only the failure half, so the deviation is untested by construction. CONV-034 likewise says THE OUTCOME (not the failure) must ride the never-silently-dropped signal.
fix: In deliver(), route a detached success through useStore.getState().pushToast(settled.message) (default suppressible success kind) alongside the existing pushToast(settled.message, error) for failures - verified compatible with frozen TEST-605 (its error-kind regex and catch-block pins stay satisfied). Record the plan deviation; add a detached-success pin where a harness permits (the e2e drawer-close path now exists).
resolution: Resolved (consolidated re-verification) - the contract blocker is closed. deliver()'s detached branch routes BOTH outcome classes through the store-level chokepoint (orky-entry-actions.tsx:87-93): the FAILURE keeps pushToast(settled.message, 'error') (never suppressed) and the SUCCESS rides pushToast(settled.message) - the DEFAULT suppressible kind, so suppression is the STORE's mechanism (toasts-slice.ts:14-20 kind !== 'error' early-return), never a call-site drop; both carry the core-classified honesty copy (CONV-034), matching the F12 OrkyCaptureModal precedent. REQ-010's MUST is honored - no outcome is silently swallowed. Pinned by amended frozen TEST-605 (a pushToast call WITHOUT the error kind carrying settled.message must exist - green against current source) + e2e TEST-623 (drawer closed mid-flight -> default-kind toast with the answered/submitted copy, exactly one dispatch). Contract violation cleared.

claim: The next-action preview renders only result.data.next and drops data.phase/data.reason, so against the REAL gatekeeper drive output it under-describes the computed next action. gk.drive returns structured objects - {next:run-phase, phase:spec}, {next:retry-phase, phase, failed, attempt}, {next:await-human, reason}, {next:escalate, ...} (gatekeeper.js:843-904) - and REQ-005 own example is (quote) next: run-phase spec (end quote), i.e. phase included. The shipped preview shows (quote) next: run-phase (end quote) with no phase (WHICH phase to run is the load-bearing datum) and (quote) next: await-human (end quote) with no reason. No test can catch this: frozen TEST-593 uses the unreal fixture data:{next:'run-phase spec'} - a single-string shape the CLI never emits - and the e2e gatekeeper stub returns only {next:await-human} (orky-queue-actions.spec.ts:77), so every pin passes while the real rendering is degraded. Honesty itself is intact (no mutation-claiming words), so this is a fidelity/test-adequacy gap, not a false claim.
fix: In settlePreview, compose the message from the structured fields when present - e.g. next: ${next}${phase ? ' ' + phase : ''} plus the reason when carried (await-human/escalate) - which stays green under frozen TEST-593/TEST-608 (message still contains data.next and /next/i, adds no forbidden word). Note for the tests lens: fixtures for an upstream CLI contract should use shapes the CLI actually emits (drive.test.js is the ground truth).
resolution: Resolved (consolidated re-verification). settlePreview composes the message from the structured gk.drive shape (orky-entry-actions-core.ts:288-292): 'next: ' + next + (phase ? ' '+phase : '') + (reason ? ' - '+reason : '') - WHICH phase to run and WHY a human is awaited render as carried; no mutation-claiming word (TEST-593's /resumed|advanced|dispatched|continued|unblock/i scan stays green). Both harnesses re-fixtured to the REAL CLI shapes: amended TEST-593 drives {next:'run-phase',phase}, {next:'await-human',reason[,phase]}, {next:'retry-phase',phase,failed,attempt}, {next:'done'} (gatekeeper.js:843-904), and the amended e2e TEST-608 gatekeeper stub answers {next:'await-human', reason:'open escalation', escalations:[...]} with the carried reason asserted in the rendered preview. All green; hashes match test-freeze.

claim: A stale settled failure masks the REQ-003 unbindable-target message: errorView gives phase.status===failure precedence over the display-time binding failure, and phase is never reset when the answer form opens. Interleaving: preview fails (phase=failure persists) -> user opens answer -> the binding pull fails -> dq-action-error shows the UNRELATED preview failure while the binding message (couldn't read this project's Orky detail / no feature matching / etc.) renders nowhere - the target span says only (quote) no escalation bound (end quote). REQ-003 requires the unbindable case to (quote) show an actionable message naming the reason (end quote); in this interleaving it does not. Dispatch stays correctly blocked (submit disabled via boundOk), so impact is a wrong-context error message, not a wrong write.
fix: Clear phase to idle when the answer form opens (in toggleAnswer, alongside bindAnswer), or invert the errorView precedence while the answer form is open with a failed binding - either keeps every frozen pin green (no test pins the precedence).
resolution: Merged into FINDING-011 — same stale-phase-masks-message defect, filed by devils-advocate(014) and quality(011); 011 canonical.

claim: REQ-010 MUST violation: a detached SUCCESS outcome is silently swallowed. deliver() routes only settled.status==='failure' through pushToast when aliveRef.current is false; the in-code comment says 'A detached success is dropped - only failures must never be swallowed', directly contradicting REQ-010's normative text ('the settled outcome MUST still be reported through the store-level toast chokepoint (pushToast)'; 'No outcome is ever silently swallowed') and CONV-034's base rule ('the outcome MUST still be reported through a component-independent, never-silently-dropped signal'). REQ-010's own acceptance names the suppressible success path as the sanctioned channel ('the store's success path is suppressible while the error path is not'), and the shipped F12 precedent (OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:106-108) pushes the detached success toast unconditionally. Consequence: submit an answer or record a verdict and close the drawer (or the row unmounts on the next aggregate refresh) before the CLI settles - the durable write lands with ZERO user-visible confirmation; on the feedback-enabled path the escalation stays open (row stays queued), so the silent success invites a duplicating re-answer. Frozen TEST-605 pins only the error-kind toast, so the suite stays green over the violation (test-adequacy gap: the success half of REQ-010 was never pinned).
fix: In deliver()'s detached branch also report success through the suppressible chokepoint - useStore.getState().pushToast(settled.message) (default kind) - mirroring OrkyCaptureModal's detached-success behavior; failures keep the never-suppressed 'error' kind. Add a pin (structural, at the deliver seam) that the detached branch routes BOTH outcome classes through pushToast, amended per CONV-019 discipline alongside frozen TEST-605.
resolution: Merged into FINDING-012 — same detached-SUCCESS-dropped REQ-010/CONV-034 violation, independently filed by networking; 012 canonical (devils-advocate).

claim: Opening the answer flow never moves keyboard focus into the inline input: toggleAnswer only flips answerOpen state, and the module contains no useOpenFocusRestore/autoFocus/.focus() call - although the spec's Verified contract explicitly names useOpenFocusRestore as the 'focus substrate to reuse ... for the inline answer input, as F12 does (OrkyCaptureModal.tsx:191)'. A keyboard user who activates dq-action-answer with Enter must then Tab across 'next?' and 'resume in terminal' to reach dq-action-answer-input; a pointer user must aim a second click at an 11px-font input inside the 340px drawer. The primary flow (answer -> type -> submit) costs extra undocumented navigation on every use. No REQ made the named substrate normative, so no frozen test pins focus landing (TEST-610 only asserts focus is not STOLEN after the user clicks the input themselves).
fix: Move focus to dq-action-answer-input (the evidence input for human-review mode) when the form opens - a ref focused in the open gesture, or useOpenFocusRestore per the spec's Verified contract. propose CONV: An inline form region revealed by an explicit open gesture MUST move keyboard focus into its first interactive field on open - the inline-form twin of CONV-020's drawer open-focus rule.
resolution: Resolved (consolidated re-verification). The inline forms are extracted (EscalationAnswerForm :208-252, HumanReviewForm :257-284) and mounted ONLY while answerOpen && mode matches (:370-384), each wiring the shared useOpenFocusRestore with its ref on the first input - focus lands in dq-action-answer-input (or dq-action-evidence) on the open GESTURE itself; closed rows mount NO substrate, so drawer open steals nothing across rows, and the wire-once effect fires exactly at form open. Pinned by TEST-616 (substrate reused, ref on the input) + e2e TEST-620 (focused testid = dq-action-answer-input after open). No frozen structural pin's expectation changes: the byte-frozen structure suite (TEST-597..606) passes against the extracted layout (verified by run - 11/11), TEST-599's useEffect scan covers only orky-entry-actions.tsx spans (the substrate's effect lives in use-open-focus-restore.ts, considered in 04-tests.md), and TEST-610's row-focus assertions hold (focus in the input is inside the actions region). One fix-introduced residual filed as FINDING-022 (LOW): a reason flip while the form is open swaps forms and moves focus without a gesture.

claim: Enter in the decision input dispatches nothing: there is no form element and no onKeyDown on dq-action-answer-input, so the canonical single-line-input submit keystroke is dead - the user must Tab (past nothing visible; the submit sits after the input) or click dq-action-answer-submit. REQ-006 itself sanctions 'the inline-input submit' as a gesture class, and frozen TEST-608 has to work AROUND the gap (submit.focus() then keyboard Enter) instead of typing Enter in the input. Typing a decision and pressing Enter - the most likely keystroke sequence in this flow - does nothing at all, reading as a broken control.
fix: Dispatch the submit on Enter inside dq-action-answer-input (target-guarded; the existing refusal conditions - empty/whitespace decision, busy, !boundOk - still gate it). Keep the evidence input Enter-free deliberately (two verdict buttons make a default ambiguous). propose CONV: A single-line inline text input paired with exactly ONE submit control MUST dispatch that submit on Enter (respecting the submit's disabled conditions), never requiring pointer or Tab traversal to complete the flow.
resolution: Resolved (consolidated re-verification). Enter submits from INSIDE the decision input via an input-scoped onKeyDown (orky-entry-actions.tsx:235-242): Enter-only, preventDefault, honoring the SAME refuse gate as the submit control (whitespace-only decision, in-flight busy, unbound target all refuse; answerEscalation re-checks trim as a second gate) - one typed Enter = one emit (the core's single-flight absorbs key-repeat). The evidence input stays deliberately Enter-free (two verdict buttons make a default ambiguous - the finding's own boundary). CONV-030 clean: the handler is the input's own, no container-level key redirect; and the code does not trip TEST-615's banned isInFlight/busy early-return pattern. Pinned by TEST-617 + e2e TEST-620 (whitespace Enter dispatches NOTHING - empty argv logs; typed Enter -> exactly ONE byte-verbatim emit + honest success).

claim: The next-action preview renders ONLY data.next and drops phase/reason, although gatekeeper drive returns richer shapes (Orky gatekeeper.js drive(): {next:'await-human', reason:'open escalation'}, {next:'run-phase', phase:'spec'}, {next:'await-human', reason:'iteration cap reached for implement (3/3)', phase:'implement'}, {next:'retry-phase', phase, failed, attempt}). The spec's own REQ-005 example is 'next: run-phase spec' - next PLUS phase. Rendered reality: every escalation/human-review queue row previews as the information-free 'next: await-human' (the row is in the queue precisely BECAUSE a human is needed - the reason is the useful part), and a stalled row previews 'next: run-phase' without naming WHICH phase. Both frozen harnesses flattened the real shape and mask the loss: TEST-593 fixtures next:'run-phase spec' as a single pre-joined string the real CLI never emits, and the TEST-608 gatekeeper stub returns {next:'await-human'} with no reason/phase - so the suite stays green while the shipped preview under-informs on every real row.
fix: Compose the preview message from next + phase + reason when present (e.g. 'next: run-phase spec', 'next: await-human - open escalation'), keeping REQ-005's no-mutation-claim scan green; correct the TEST-593 fixture and TEST-608 stub to the real drive output shape in the same CONV-019-disciplined amendment.
resolution: Merged into FINDING-013 — same preview-drops-phase/reason gap, filed by ux(018) and devils-advocate(013); 013 canonical.

claim: Outcome announcement is asymmetric for assistive technology: dq-action-error carries role='alert' (announced), but dq-action-result and dq-action-pending are plain divs with no role='status'/aria-live - a screen-reader user who submits an answer hears a failure but never the pending state or the success/preview outcome, so the honest success copy REQ-008 mandates is invisible to AT and the flow ends in silence on the good path.
fix: Add role='status' (a polite live region) to dq-action-result and dq-action-pending so success, preview output, and the in-flight state are announced like the error already is.
resolution: Resolved (consolidated re-verification). dq-action-pending (:367) and dq-action-result (:386) both carry role="status" (polite live regions) while dq-action-error keeps role="alert" (:392) - pending, success and preview outcomes are now announced to AT symmetrically with failures. Pinned by TEST-618.

claim: After a successful answer the form stays fully armed: answerOpen stays true, the decision text persists, and dq-action-answer-submit re-enables (busy false) directly beside the 'Escalation answered - the decision was submitted.' result. On the feedback-enabled path the escalation stays OPEN until Orky applies the queued decision, so the row remains in the queue with reason 'escalation' AND the submit-time re-verification (REQ-003) will PASS - one more click re-dispatches the same decision and durably queues a DUPLICATE, exactly the duplicate class REQ-009's indeterminate copy warns against, here invited by the success path itself. The mixed signal (success message + row still queued + armed form) makes that second click likely for a user checking whether the answer 'took'. (On the gatekeeper fallback path the re-verify refuses, so the exposure is specifically the feedback-enabled deferred-apply window.)
fix: Disarm on success: clear the decision text and close (or disable) the answer form until the user re-opens it (re-opening already re-binds correctly), and/or extend the success copy to say the entry stays queued until Orky applies the decision. propose CONV: After a non-idempotent submit succeeds, the submitting affordance MUST be disarmed (input cleared, form closed, or submit disabled) until the user re-arms it - a still-armed form holding the same payload is a duplicate-write invite.
resolution: Resolved (consolidated re-verification). The phase-keyed disarm effect (orky-entry-actions.tsx:319-325) closes the form and clears decision + evidence when phase settles {status:'success', action:'answer'} - the armed same-payload re-fire invite is gone on exactly the feedback-path window the finding named. No loop/re-fire on re-render: deps [phase], and the effect's setters (setAnswerOpen/setDecision/setEvidence) never touch phase, so the effect runs once per settled phase identity; re-open calls resetPhase (idle) so it no-ops, and re-arming is an explicit gesture that re-binds with an EMPTY input. Scoped to action==='answer' so a preview success never closes the form. Pinned by e2e TEST-621 (input unmounted after success; re-open shows ESC-007 bound with empty value; feedback log still exactly length 1).

claim: REQ-003 submit-time re-verification leaves an UNDISCLOSED residual TOCTOU window: the verify pull runs renderer-side BEFORE the dispatch enters F7 per-feature serialization (queue.run at orky-action-dispatcher.ts:160), so between verify and the actual CLI write sits the whole queue wait plus the CLI startup - and Orky's gatekeeper resolveEscalation (gatekeeper.js:758-767) finds by id and overwrites status/decision/resolvedAt UNCONDITIONALLY, with no open-status refusal; the feedback path likewise queues a decision event with no openness check. An escalation resolved elsewhere in that window (watchdog, another window, an Orky session) is silently RE-resolved, overwriting the earlier decision text, and the user sees an honest-looking success. The identity guarantee holds (the write lands on the id the user read), REQ-003 letter is met (the verify IS performed), and the impl cannot do better client-side - but unlike REQ-007, which explicitly disclaims the cross-window duplicate the mechanism cannot prevent, the spec never discloses this residual race, and two spec reviews (FINDING-003 repair) stopped the analysis at the verify. Real fix is upstream: gatekeeper refusing to resolve a non-open escalation without an explicit force, or F7 performing the open-check inside the serialized critical section.
fix: Record the residual window as a disclosed limitation in the spec/docs (the REQ-007-style honesty note), and file the upstream improvement against Orky (gatekeeper resolve-escalation should refuse a non-open escalation absent --force; F7 could then surface it verbatim as a definite cli-error). propose CONV: A client-side pre-write verification guarding a write that has no server-side compare-and-set MUST either be enforced inside the write's own serialized critical section or the spec MUST explicitly disclose the residual verify-to-write race window.
note: Re-verified 2026-07-02T23:10:37.377Z (consolidated review): stays an ACCEPTED, documented residual per ESC-001 - recorded in 04-tests.md LOOPBACK 1 ("FINDING-021 ... ACCEPTED residual ... deliberately NOT pinned"; the upstream gatekeeper refuse-non-open-without-force improvement is filed against Orky). LOW, non-blocking; intentionally left open as the disclosed limitation.

claim: The FINDING-016 fix mounts the open-focus substrate on FORM MOUNT, but the form mount is keyed on answerOpen && mode and answerOpen is never reset when the target's reason flips. Queue rows are keyed by the stable featureSlug (DecisionQueuePanel.tsx:171/:190), so the OrkyEntryActions instance survives aggregate refreshes: with the escalation answer form open, a background refresh that flips it.status.reason escalation -> human-review (the escalation resolves elsewhere while the human-review gate becomes pending) unmounts EscalationAnswerForm and mounts HumanReviewForm, whose useOpenFocusRestore moves keyboard focus into dq-action-evidence with NO user gesture - the data-driven focus theft class CONV-020 exists to prevent (the user may be mid-typing in an xterm pane). The swap also silently re-purposes the open state: the form opened for a decision now shows verdict buttons. Narrow interleaving, no wrong write (the decision text is retained but hidden, and dispatch gates hold), hence LOW.
fix: Reset answerOpen (and the decision/evidence text) when mode changes while the form is open - a mode-keyed effect mirroring the FINDING-020 disarm - so a reason flip CLOSES the stale form instead of swapping it under the focus substrate; focus then only ever moves on the explicit toggle gesture. propose CONV: A focus-on-mount substrate may only be mounted by an explicit user open gesture - a data-driven remount or surface swap MUST NOT move keyboard focus.
